text: The surgeon attempts to open the retroperitoneum, revealing loops of bowel adherent to the area. Upon further dissection, a greenish biliary appearance is observed, indicating an inadvertent injury to the small bowel.

The surgeon uses scissors to make sharp cuts, freeing the bowel from retroperitoneal attachments to localize the injury site. The appendix is visible in the lower frame, and the injury is identified on the ileum, approximately three to four centimeters proximal to the ileocecal junction. The mucosal layer of the bowel injury is sutured with Vicryl in a continuous interlocking manner, ensuring proper approximation to prevent stricturing.

The surgeon approximates the mucosal layer of the small bowel injury using absorbable suture material in a continuous manner. Following this, the second layer is closed with 3-0 silk on a round body needle, using interrupted sutures to reduce tension and reinforce the mucosal layer.
The surgeon ties a patch of omentum over the suture line to reinforce the closure after approximating the second layer of the bowel repair.

The surgeon completes the closure of the second layer of the bowel repair, placing a patch of omentum over the suture line for reinforcement. The final view shows the completed repair with the omentum patch in place.
